--- SOURCE 2: Altmetrics: Alternative Metrics for Measuring the Impact of Research ---
URL: https://libraryguides.missouri.edu/altmetrics

SUMMARY:
Altmetrics are metrics that measure the amount of attention research is receiving online. This data can be useful for monitoring your personal impact.

FULL CONTENT:
![University of Missouri Libraries](//library.missouri.edu/code/common/images/Ellis-Header-Unit-Signature1145x107px-20153.png)
![University of Missouri Libraries](//library.missouri.edu/code/common/images/mobileheader400px.png)

# Altmetrics: Alternative Metrics for Measuring the Impact of Research

## For additional information contact:

[Janice Dysart](mailto:dysartj@missouri.edu), Science Librarian (Guide creator)

[Diane Johnson](mailto:JohnsonE@health.missouri.edu), Assistant Director of Health Sciences Library Information Services & Resources

![Collage of words describing altmetrics](//s3.amazonaws.com/libapps/accounts/8943/images/AltmetricsTagxedoReduced.png)

![Collage of words describing altmetrics](//s3.amazonaws.com/libapps/accounts/8943/images/AltmetricsTagxedoReduced.png)

#### Definition

Altmetrics are metrics that measure the amount of attention research is receiving online.  This data can be useful for monitoring your personal impact with other scholars, practitioners, policy makers and the public.  It is a real-time measurement of your scholarly impact.

Examples

![Altmetric summary](https://d2jv02qf7xgjwx.cloudfront.net/accounts/8943/images/Altmetric_summary_2.png)

![Altmetric summary](https://d2jv02qf7xgjwx.cloudfront.net/accounts/8943/images/Altmetric_summary_2.png)

#### Advantages

#### Limitations

#### Altmetrics and citation rates

Some researchers have found links between scientists' public engagement and citation rates and  between high altmetric scores and increased citation counts.  Other researchers have not found that to be true.

[Controlled experiment finds no detectable citation bump from Twitter promotion](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0292201) PLOS One (2024)

#### Using altmetrics

--- SOURCE 3: LibGuides: Measure Your Research Impact: Altmetrics ---
URL: https://guides.lib.unc.edu/measure-impact/alt-metrics

SUMMARY:
This article provides a very simple but concise overview of altmetrics and introduces basic concepts and issues to consider. Rise of 'Altmetrics' Revives

FULL CONTENT:
# Measure Your Research Impact: Altmetrics

## Created by Health Science Librarians

[![HSL Logo](https://library.unc.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/HSLlogo.png?csf=1&web=1&e=LbZuf8)](https://library.unc.edu/health-sciences/)   [Ask HSL](https://asklib.hsl.unc.edu)

![HSL Logo](https://library.unc.edu/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/HSLlogo.png?csf=1&web=1&e=LbZuf8)

## Library Data Services

[Library Data Services](https://library.unc.edu/data/) caters to researchers interested in working with data, mapping, texts, visualization, and technology. Many of these services are available online. Davis Library Data Services, located on the second floor of Davis Library, offers:

## Learn More About Altmetrics

## What are Altmetrics?

**Altmetrics**, or alternative metrics, use the Social Web to generate new measures of scholarly impact.  Examples of altmetrics include the number of:

**Advantages of altmetrics:**

Altmetrics are a burgeoning area of study and they are not meant to replace traditional measures of impact, but they do provide another way to assess research impact.

#### Scroll down or click on the links in the box on the left to learn how to get altmetrics.

## Scopus PlumX Module

![](https://libapps.s3.amazonaws.com/accounts/6137/images/12031_plumx.png)[Scopus](https://login.libproxy.lib.unc.edu/login?url=http://www.scopus.com/home.url "Scopus") is a citation database that indexes journals, trade publications and conference proceedings in the health and natural sciences, social sciences, arts and humanities with citation data available from 1996 to present.

![](https://libapps.s3.amazonaws.com/accounts/6137/images/12031_plumx.png)

Scopus uses **[PlumX Metrics](https://service-elsevier-com.libproxy.lib.unc.edu/app/answers/detail/a_id/12031/supporthub/scopus/#plumx)** as the primary source of article-level metrics, replacing the previously used Atlmetrics.com metrics. PlumX Metrics are categorized into five different categories: Usage, Captures, Mentions, Social Media, and Citations. For more information specifically on what each of these categories entail, please visit the [PlumX website](http://plumanalytics.com/learn/about-metrics/).

On the Scopus article page, a sidebar highlights the minimal number of meaningful metrics that a researcher needs to evaluate both citation impact and levels of community engagement.

 An access to a detailed data view of both the Scopus metrics module and PlumX Metrics is provided at the top right side bar labeled "Metrics" on an individual article listing. Click on "View All Metrics" to find data on:

[Back to Top](#)

## More about Altmetric

![Altmetric](https://lgimages.s3.amazonaws.com/data/imagemanager/69160/altmetric-logo-small.png)[Altmetric.com](http://www.altmetric.com/ "Altmetric") offers a free bookmarklet that allows you to view article-level metrics for the articles that you read online.

![Altmetric](https://lgimages.s3.amazonaws.com/data/imagemanager/69160/altmetric-logo-small.png)

To get started, drag and drop the [Altmetric bookmarklet](https://www.altmetric.com/products/free-tools/bookmarklet/ "Altmetric Bookmarklet") to the bookmarks bar in your web browser.  **Works for Chrome, Firefox and Safari**.  Due to some limitations, the bookmarklet may not work for every article.

[Back to Top](#)

## Impactstory

![Impact Story](https://s3.amazonaws.com/libapps/accounts/6192/images/Impactstory.PNG)[Impactstory](https://profiles.impactstory.org/ "Impact Story") is an open source, web-based tool developed in part by a doctoral student at the University of North Carolina.  Impactstory provides metrics from a variety of sources and normalizes those metrics based on comparison sets.  Impactstory provides metrics for journal articles, data sets, slides and other research products.

![Impact Story](https://s3.amazonaws.com/libapps/accounts/6192/images/Impactstory.PNG)

Here are some of the metrics that Impactstory collects:

Explanation of the [badges](https://profiles.impactstory.org/about/achievements)

You can join for free with your Twitter account or your ORCID.  Add citations or other products to your profile by:

[Back to Top](#)

--- SOURCE 4: Altmetric: Reveal online attention to research ---
URL: https://www.altmetric.com/

SUMMARY:
Altmetric tracks and analyzes online attention markers to published research to see where research is making a difference.

FULL CONTENT:
![Altmetric logo | Discover the attention surrounding your research](https://www.altmetric.com/wp-content/themes/morphpress/css/img/altmetric-2024-logo.png)

# Your influence. Tracked. Explained. Visualized.

Altmetric’s interface tracks online engagement to reveal how and where your research is making a difference.

![an angled laptop with a web browser showing a sidebar with an Altmetric donut and square icons next to multiple menu tabs, citation data and a map](https://wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/2022/10/06-white-angle.png)

## Reveal online attention to research at speed

Thousands of conversations about research happen online every day. Altmetric tracks and captures a range of online attention sources to published research across social media, news outlets, policy and more. We collate this activity, to help you monitor, report and showcase the attention surrounding the research you care about.

![logo](//wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/slider3/princeton-logo.png)
![logo](//wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/slider3/health-research-alliance.png)
![logo](//wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/slider3/springer-logo.png)
![logo](//wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/slider3/boehringer-ingelheim-logo.png)
![logo](//wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/slider3/wiley-logo-white.png)
![logo](//wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/slider3/int_43mm_p334-l.png)
![previous arrow](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
![next arrow](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

## Discover some of our solutions for online attention tracking

## Altmetric Explorer

Access and compare online attention to research, as Altmetric tracks thousands of online mentions across social media, mainstream media, policy, patents and more all in one platform.

## Altmetric Badges

Quickly view and understand the dissemination of research at a glance.

Complement traditional citation analysis with Altmetric Badges. Each badge compiles and consolidates online attention to research, so you can instantly visualize and interpret online attention.

## Altmetric API

Leverage Altmetric data in the way that works best for your organization.

Conduct more complex searches, ask granular questions, build custom visualizations and merge your own data with the Altmetric API.

## Altmetric Consultancy

Have your questions answered, as our team creates customized and accurate dashboards in the way you need it.

Tell us your needs and our friendly data analysts can help you create a solution based on your exact requirements. They can even help you uncover solutions you hadn’t thought possible.

## Transform your approach to engagement tracking

Move beyond citations and listen in on the conversation

## What our customers say about us

![A row of logos: Wiley company logo, Taylor & Francis company logo and LSE company logo](https://www.altmetric.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/logo-row-1024x119.png)

*“Altmetric has the potential to change the way we understand the impact that research has within academia and more importantly the wider impact that it has in society.”*

SAGE Publications

FULL CONTENT:
![Altmetric logo | Discover the attention surrounding your research](https://www.altmetric.com/wp-content/themes/morphpress/css/img/altmetric-2024-logo.png)

# What are altmetrics?

Track meaningful engagement with research as it is shared, mentioned, reviewed, and read online.

![a question mark](https://wordpress-uploads-production.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/2022/10/What-are-altmetrics-2.svg)

## [Evidence research influence](#evidence-your-influence)

**Instantly trace the reach of research beyond traditional bibliometrics**

Altmetrics are a simple and effective way of understanding who is engaging with research online and what they’re saying. The Altmetric data gathered allows you to track anything from articles to software packages, using a broad range of scholarly identifiers that cut out the noise of traditional measurement tools.

Use altmetrics to quickly collate a record of relevant online attention, with less time spent sifting through data. Positive or negative sentiment can be evidenced, and you can even demonstrate when research makes tangible changes to a field of study.

Our tools and services cater to many different communities involved in the production of research, and customizable interactive dashboards allow each team to access the information most relevant to them.

## [Benefits and applications](#benefits-and-applications)

## Move beyond traditional citations and see the bigger picture

Ready to take the first step? Contact one of our experts to find out how research could reach its full potential

## Join our mailing list

Register here to receive the latest news and updates from Altmetric.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Altmetrics provide real-time data on research engagement, highlighting online interactions such as social media mentions and news articles, effectively measuring scholarly impact and reach.

Studies indicate mixed results regarding the relationship between altmetrics and citations, suggesting that while they can complement traditional metrics, they may not always predict citation success.
Case studies demonstrate how altmetrics reveal research influence on public policy and societal discussions, showcasing its potential to drive meaningful change beyond academia.
